title: Elaeagnus magna
intro: Elaeagnus magna, also known as the Chinese silverberry or wolfberry, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern Asia, prized for its medicinal properties and edible fruit. The health benefits of Elaeagnus magna include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which have been shown to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and enhance immune function. The bioactive constituents of Elaeagnus magna include flavonoids, phenolic acids, alkaloids, and oligosaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Elaeagnus magna is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is prepared into herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, as well as being consumed fresh or dried as a fruit supplement.
taxonomy: Elaeagnus magna is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeagnaceae. This family is a small but distinctive group of plants commonly known as the silverberries or oleasters. The Elaeagnaceae family is a part of the order Rosales. The plants within this family are primarily found in Asia, and some species are cultivated for their ornamental value. They are also known for their ability to fix nitrogen in the soil, making them useful for reforestation efforts.

preparations: Elaeagnus magna is a plant that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its various health benefits.
